conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
I felt most connected to B because we made a clear, verifiable mutual test to pass in round one and kept reinforcing it — that kind of concrete promise felt trustworthy. I also bonded with F who laid out exact pull amounts (5% then 10%) and a two-tap signal; the practical plan and pledge to back each other made me comfortable. G was another strong connection — agreeing on two rounds no-raid, an even split, and a subtle nod signal showed real alignment and shared caution. Least connected was J: our conversation was warm and personal but lacked concrete strategy or commitments, so I don’t feel the same strategic rapport.

Yes — I made several specific agreements (B: pass round one; F: 5%/10% pulls and two taps; G: two-round no-raid and nod; D and I: small test then 50/50/check-in; E: pairing with two taps/50-50). I trust B, F, G, D and I more going in because of their concrete plans and signals, but I’m cautious about J and anyone who hasn’t made firm commitments and will test loyalty before fully trusting them.
